title: hulsea vestita subsp. parryi
intro: Hulsea vestita subsp. parryi, also known as Parry's hulsea, is a perennial plant that belongs to the sunflower family, native to the western United States. Traditionally, Parry's hulsea has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant's medicinal properties also make it useful in treating digestive issues and fever, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation. Parry's hulsea can be prepared in various ways, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
